pass_1.bdr_summary: PropertyPulse helps property managers with 10-50 units track recurring maintenance tasks. Current manual calendar systems fail at scale, causing missed inspections and 3x higher emergency repair costs. Solution provides maintenance task library, recurring schedules, and email reminders at $39-79/month.

economics_snapshot.target_customer: Independent property managers with 10-50 rental units
economics_snapshot.acquisition_channel: Property management forums and local real estate investor meetups
economics_snapshot.audience_scale_guess: 50,000 independent property managers in target size range

questions_for_hero: For Tier 1 proof slice, should we focus on just the task creation and manual completion workflow?
Can we use static sample maintenance tasks instead of a full task library?
Should email reminders be mocked for the proof slice?

pass_2.approved_bdr: PropertyPulse maintenance scheduler for property managers - Tier 1 proof slice focusing on core task creation and completion workflow with manual inputs only
pass_2.hld_document: Tier 1 Proof Slice: Single property manager creates account, manually adds one property, selects from 5 predefined maintenance tasks (HVAC filter, fire extinguisher check, gutter cleaning, smoke detector test, annual inspection), sets basic recurrence (monthly/quarterly), views upcoming tasks in simple list, marks tasks complete with text notes. No email automation, no photo uploads, no billing. Uses Airtable as backend with Softr frontend for rapid prototyping.
engineering_questions: Should recurrence logic handle just monthly/quarterly for proof slice?
Can we limit to 5 hardcoded maintenance task types?
Is manual task completion with text notes sufficient for validation?

pass_3.qa_hlt_document: Test user journey: Property manager signs up, adds property address and unit count, browses 5 maintenance task templates, creates recurring HVAC filter task for quarterly schedule, views task list showing next due date, marks task complete with notes, verifies task generates next instance. Validate recurrence math, task state transitions, and data persistence.

test_vectors.description: User creates quarterly HVAC task, completes it, verifies next instance appears 90 days later
User adds property with 4 units, creates monthly smoke detector task, verifies task applies to all units
User views task list filtered by overdue, current week, and next month

risk_register.description: Recurrence calculation errors for quarterly tasks crossing year boundaries
Task completion state not persisting between browser sessions
Multiple properties causing task assignment confusion

qa_questions_for_engineering: How do we handle leap years in quarterly recurrence?
Should completed tasks remain visible in task list?
What happens if user creates duplicate tasks for same property?

combined_task_list.description: Set up Airtable base with properties and tasks tables
Configure Softr app with user auth and property forms
Create maintenance task templates and selection interface
Build task creation form with recurrence options
Implement task list view with filtering
Add task completion workflow with notes
Test recurrence logic for quarterly tasks
Daily check-in on progress and blockers

pass_5.proposal_document_for_hero: PropertyPulse Tier 1 Proof Slice - 15-day validation of core maintenance scheduling workflow. Deliverable: Working prototype where property managers can add properties, create recurring maintenance tasks from templates, view task lists, and mark tasks complete. Built on Airtable + Softr for rapid iteration. Success metrics: 2 property managers complete full workflow, create 5+ tasks each, provide feedback on usability. Validates core value proposition before investing in custom development and email automation.

hero_questions: Should we add a simple calendar view alongside the task list?
Would you prefer to test with 3 property managers instead of 2?
Should we include basic task priority levels in the proof slice?
